domains_and_variables: G is a finite bipartite graph with left part L and right part R
definitions: A matching saturates L if every vertex of L is incident to exactly one matching edge
N(S) is the set of neighbors in R of vertices in S
hypotheses: For every subset S of L, |N(S)| >= |S|
target_formalization.claim: G has a matching saturating L

degeneracy_checks_passed: Singleton L follows from Hall condition

external_dependencies.exact_statement: A finite bipartite graph has a matching saturating L iff every subset S of L satisfies |N(S)| >= |S|.
required_preconditions: G is bipartite with parts L and R
Hall condition holds for every subset S of L

lemma_plan.statement: G is finite bipartite with parts L and R, and Hall condition holds.
The preconditions of Hall's marriage theorem are satisfied.
lemma_plan.method: definition unfolding of finite bipartite graph and Hall condition

lemma_plan.statement: G has a matching saturating L.
